reasoning: As of late February 2025, 48 African countries had ratified the AfCFTA, an increase of one from the 47 reported throughout much of 2024. However, as of January 30, 2025, South Sudan was explicitly listed by multiple sources as one of the seven remaining signatories that had not yet ratified. Given the lack of any specific news confirming South Sudan's ratification between February and the forecast date of May 2, 2025, it is unlikely that South Sudan was the 48th country or has ratified since. Furthermore, South Sudan is currently facing significant domestic challenges, including an economic crisis and the postponement of national elections to 2026, which likely deprioritize the legislative process for international trade agreements. With only two months remaining until the July 1, 2025 deadline and no evidence of the bill being active in the Transitional National Legislative Assembly, the probability of ratification is low.
